ADF: Automatic Document Feeder, a feature in printers and scanners that allows multiple pages to be fed into the machine automatically.
AIO: All-In-One, a device that combines multiple functions, such as printing, scanning, and copying.
CDM: Common Data Model, a standardized data model used for data integration and analytics.
CMYK: Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, and Key (Black) - the four colors used in color printing
CPK: Capability Process Index, a statistical measure of process capability
EE: Electrical Engineering, a field of engineering focused on electrical systems.
engineDiagnosticsData: Engine Diagnostics Data, data collected for diagnosing the performance of a device's engine.
engineStat: Engine Status, a term used to describe the status of a device's engine.
EPEAT3: Electronic Product Environmental Assessment Tool (specific program/version reference)
eventingEvent: An event within a system's eventing framework.
frontPanelFlow: Flow of data or operations in a device's front panel.
fwupdate: Firmware Update, the process of updating a device's firmware.
HW: Hardware, the physical components of a device.
iotOnboarding: Internet of Things Onboarding, the process of setting up and connecting IoT devices.
ISO: International Organization for Standardization, an international standard-setting body.
LOT4: A term related to compliance and certification standards.
Lynx (aka Lebi Duplex): Lynx is also known as Lebi Duplex
Magi: A specific type of ink used in the project
mechDiagnosticsData: Mechanical Diagnostics Data, data collected for diagnosing mechanical performance.
MM: Monitoring Module, used for system performance monitoring.
Nessie: A specific type of ink used in the project
NOA-F: Part of a print head assembly
OOBE: Out-Of-Box Experience, the initial experience a user has when setting up a new device.
OPP: Opening Price Point, the lowest price at which a product is offered
Ops: Operations, the activities involved in running a business or system.
PDL: Page Description Language, a language used to describe the layout and contents of a printed page.
PHA: Print Head Assembly, a component of a printer that contains the print heads.
PPM: Pages Per Minute, a measure of printer speed.
PQ: Print Quality, a measure of the quality of printed output.
SHAID: Technology used to detect ink level in a reservoir
SKU: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for each distinct product and service that can be purchased.
